title: Palette of Narmer
topic_path: Predynastic, Early Dynastic, and Old Kingdom

assessment_items.question_content:
**The horizontal bands that organize the imagery in the *Palette of Narmer* are called $\_\_\_\_\_\_$ **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/59035336f51f87d5c5918045c943987d.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The palette is divided into horizontal registers. 

**[Read this article to review.](/humanities/ancient-art-civilizations/egypt-art/predynastic-old-kingdom/a/palette-of-king-narmer)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**All of the following are associated with the King's regalia EXCEPT**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
In ancient Egypt, kings wore a crown, a kilt, a false beard, and a bull tail.  This regalia continued to signify the Egyptian king's royal status until the Roman era.

**[Read this article to review.](/humanities/ancient-art-civilizations/egypt-art/predynastic-old-kingdom/a/palette-of-king-narmer)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Recent research on the decorative program of  the *Palette of Narmer* suggests it may depict    [[☃ dropdown 1]]  or   [[☃ dropdown 2]]**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/59035336f51f87d5c5918045c943987d.jpg)
hints: Older interpretations suggest the scene depicts the historical unification of Upper and Lower Egypt or refers to the general concept of unification.

The most recent scholars posit that this imagery depicts the daily journey of the sun or is meant to refer to the balance of chaos and order (ma'at and isfet) that was a fundamental element of the Egyptian cosmos. 

**[Read this article to review.](/humanities/ancient-art-civilizations/egypt-art/predynastic-old-kingdom/a/palette-of-king-narmer)**

choices.content: the daily journey of the sun
the king's journey to the afterlife
the mythical journey to the sphinx
the triumph of good over evil
the unification of Upper and Lower Egypt
the balance of chaos and order

assessment_items.question_content:
**How is King Narmer identified in this processional scene?**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1cc04c2686773376f7ec9e9a789b44f0.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Narmer appears in Mace-Pose (or smiting pose) on the opposite side of the Palette where he's shown defeating his enemy (and not on the side illustrated here). However, Narmer does wear the crown of lower Egypt, a bull tail, and he is the largest figure.


**[Read this article to review.](/humanities/ancient-art-civilizations/egypt-art/predynastic-old-kingdom/a/palette-of-king-narmer)**

choices.content: He wears the crown of lower Egypt.
He is depicted in the Mace-Pose (or smiting pose)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What helps to distinguish the *Palette of Narmer* as a ceremonial object?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The *Palette of Narmer* was discovered buried with other ritual objects beneath an ancient temple to the god Horus.

This sculpture is larger and more elaborate than ordinary palettes, which were used to grind and hold eye makeup that helped reduce glare from the sun.

**[Read this article to review.](/humanities/ancient-art-civilizations/egypt-art/predynastic-old-kingdom/a/palette-of-king-narmer)**

choices.content: The decorative carving on the object
The function of the object for grinding and mixing eye makeup
The site where it was found

assessment_items.question_content:
**What theme seems most central in the *Palette of Narmer*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Although it's not clear whether the scenes represent historical events, the date of the sculpture and its depiction of the same ruler wearing crowns of both Upper and Lower Egypt reflect the theme of unification.  

**[Read this article to review.](/humanities/ancient-art-civilizations/egypt-art/predynastic-old-kingdom/a/palette-of-king-narmer)**
